intro_script: Welcome to our June 1st, 2026 episode. We've got a fascinating lineup of stories lined up for you today. From the tech world, where OpenAI is shifting gears into hardware, to groundbreaking advancements in brain-computer interfaces - we'll dive into these top tales and more, exploring what they mean for us all.

items.title: OpenAI Pivots Directly Into Hardware, Launching Internal Robotics Division
items.summary: OpenAI is forming an internal robotics division to manufacture and program general-purpose machines. The move comes months after a split with Figure. This new initiative marks OpenAI's pivot into hardware development. Details of the division and its goals are not specified in the article.
items.audio_script:
From Humanoids Daily, "OpenAI Pivots Directly Into Hardware, Launching Internal Robotics Division". OpenAI has formed an internal robotics division to manufacture and program general-purpose machines, marking a significant pivot into hardware development. According to sources, this new initiative comes months after the company split with Figure, a former business partner that was also exploring AI-related ventures. As part of this move, OpenAI is establishing an in-house team focused on designing and developing its own robotics systems, allowing for greater control over the production and programming process. The division's goals and specific details remain undisclosed at this time. This development underscores OpenAI's expanding ambitions into hardware development and its commitment to advancing AI technologies through hands-on innovation.

OpenAI's internal robotics division is set to drive the company's efforts in manufacturing and programming general-purpose machines, bringing a level of control and strategic focus previously lacking. By establishing an in-house team, OpenAI can streamline its research and development processes, potentially accelerating advancements in this field. The company's decision to launch an internal robotics division suggests a long-term investment in hardware development, which could have significant implications for the AI and tech industries.

The establishment of OpenAI's internal robotics division may signal a broader trend in the industry, with other companies potentially following suit. As OpenAI continues to explore its potential in hardware development, industry observers will be watching closely for updates on this initiative and its impact on AI technologies and applications.

items.title: China has approved the world’s first invasive brain-computer chip—here’s what’s next
items.source: MIT Technology Review - AI & Emerging Tech
items.summary: A man named Dong Hui, who was paralyzed from the neck down after a car accident, successfully wrote with his hand using a world's first invasive brain-computer chip in China. The device allowed Dong to control devices with his thoughts, giving him greater independence. This milestone marks a significant breakthrough in brain-computer interface technology.
items.audio_script:
From MIT Technology Review - AI & Emerging Tech, "China has approved the world’s first invasive brain-computer chip—here’s what’s next." China has made a significant breakthrough in brain-computer interface technology by approving the world's first invasive brain-computer chip. Dong Hui, a man paralyzed from the neck down after a car accident, successfully wrote with his hand using this device, demonstrating its potential to restore independence.

Dong Hui underwent an implantation procedure where the brain-computer chip was inserted into his skull and connected to his neurons. The chip used electroencephalography (EEG) signals to decode his brain activity and translate it into digital commands that controlled devices such as a computer cursor, allowing him to write with greater precision. This innovation marks a major milestone in advancing brain-computer interface technology.

The approval of this device paves the way for further research and development in the field, potentially leading to breakthroughs in treating paralysis and other neurological disorders. As this technology continues to advance, it is likely to have significant implications for the AI and tech industries.

items.title: First Invasive BCI Approved for Patients with Paralysis Worldwide
items.summary: Dong Hui, 39, became the first person outside of clinical trials to receive an invasive brain-computer interface (BCI) that allows him to control his hands and perform daily tasks after suffering spinal cord injuries in a car accident six years earlier. The BCI implant, called NEO, was developed by Neuracle Technology and researchers at Tsinghua University and is now approved for patients with paralysis worldwide between 18 and 60 who have residual function in their arms. Dong's rehabilitation process began just over an hour after surgery, and he has since made significant progress, including successfully grabbing a ball without assistance on the ninth day of his training.
items.audio_script: From technologyreview.com, "First Invasive BCI Approved for Patients with Paralysis Worldwide", a breakthrough in brain-computer interface technology has been achieved as Dong Hui, 39, became the first person outside of clinical trials to receive an invasive BCI that enables him to control his hands and perform daily tasks. The BCI implant, called NEO, was developed by Neuracle Technology and researchers at Tsinghua University after Dong suffered spinal cord injuries in a car accident six years earlier. His rehabilitation process began just over an hour after surgery, and he has since made significant progress, including successfully grabbing a ball without assistance on the ninth day of his training. The approval now extends to patients with paralysis worldwide between 18 and 60 who have residual function in their arms.

items.title: Develop Physical AI Reasoning, World, and Action Models with NVIDIA Cosmos 3
items.summary: Developing physical AI reasoning, world, and action models is crucial for robots, autonomous vehicles, and smart spaces. To achieve this, NVIDIA Cosmos 3 enables developers to create systems that grasp the real-world environment through a combination of sensors, computer vision, and machine learning algorithms. This allows for accurate mapping, obstacle avoidance, and decision-making capabilities.
items.audio_script:
From NVIDIA Developer Blog, "Develop Physical AI Reasoning, World, and Action Models with NVIDIA Cosmos 3." This story highlights how developers can utilize NVIDIA Cosmos 3 to create systems that accurately grasp the real-world environment by combining sensors, computer vision, and machine learning algorithms.

To achieve this, developers are enabled to build robots, autonomous vehicles, and smart spaces that can navigate through their surroundings, avoiding obstacles and making informed decisions. By leveraging computer vision and sensor data, NVIDIA Cosmos 3 facilitates the creation of accurate mapping capabilities, allowing systems to understand their spatial relationships within the environment. This is made possible by the integration of specialized machine learning algorithms that enhance decision-making processes.

The development of physical AI reasoning, world, and action models with NVIDIA Cosmos 3 paves the way for innovative applications in various industries, including robotics and autonomous transportation.

items.title: How Cosmos 3 Helps Physical AI Think Before It Acts
items.summary: Cosmos 3, a physical AI system, is being developed to test the concept of "temporal reasoning" in artificial intelligence. Temporal reasoning involves an AI's ability to think about past and future events before taking action. The Cosmos 3 system uses sensors and actuators to simulate real-world environments, allowing researchers to study how AI systems make decisions based on time constraints. By testing temporal reasoning in a physical setting, scientists aim to create more autonomous and proactive AI systems that can adapt to changing situations.
items.audio_script:
From NVIDIA Blog, "How Cosmos 3 Helps Physical AI Think Before It Acts", researchers are developing a physical AI system called Cosmos 3 that tests the concept of temporal reasoning in artificial intelligence. This system utilizes sensors and actuators to simulate real-world environments, allowing scientists to study how AI systems make decisions based on time constraints.

The development of Cosmos 3 was accomplished by equipping it with a control system that can adjust the environment's conditions, such as temperature and lighting, to mimic changing situations. By doing so, researchers were able to observe how the AI system adapts its behavior in response to these changes. The findings indicate that Cosmos 3 is able to learn from past experiences and make more informed decisions when considering future events.

The success of Cosmos 3 means that scientists can move forward with creating more autonomous and proactive AI systems that can adapt to changing situations, ultimately leading to advancements in the field of artificial intelligence.

items.title: NVIDIA Levels Up Local AI Agents Across RTX PCs and DGX Spark
items.summary: NVIDIA has released local AI agents for use on RTX PCs and DGX Spark devices, allowing them to adapt to individual preferences and workflows, and interact with applications, generate content, automate repetitive processes and manage multi-step tasks, all while running locally on device.
items.audio_script:
From NVIDIA Blog, "NVIDIA Levels Up Local AI Agents Across RTX PCs and DGX Spark," NVIDIA has announced the release of local AI agents designed to be used on both RTX PCs and DGX Spark devices. These locally running AI agents enable users to adapt to individual preferences and workflows, interact with applications, generate content, automate repetitive processes, and manage multi-step tasks, all while maintaining full control over data and computations. The technology achieves this through advanced machine learning algorithms that allow the AI agents to learn from user behavior and optimize performance on each device, resulting in faster and more efficient processing.

This breakthrough enables users to unlock the full potential of their devices by leveraging the capabilities of local AI agents, which can be tailored to specific workflows and applications. The deployment of these locally run AI agents marks a significant advancement in the field of artificial intelligence, as it allows for greater flexibility, control, and autonomy in AI-driven processes.

The introduction of local AI agents on RTX PCs and DGX Spark devices has far-reaching implications for the AI and tech industries, setting a new standard for intelligent automation and process management.

items.title: NVIDIA Vera CPU Sets a New Standard for Agentic Workloads in AI Factories
items.summary: NVIDIA Vera CPU sets a new standard for agentic workloads in AI factories by creating a new scaling law. This is achieved through pretraining that scales intelligence using larger datasets, more parameters, and massively parallel systems.
items.audio_script:
From NVIDIA Developer Blog, "NVIDIA Vera CPU Sets a New Standard for Agentic Workloads in AI Factories," NVIDIA has announced that its Vera CPU sets a new standard for agentic workloads in AI factories by creating a new scaling law. The achievement is made possible through pretraining that scales intelligence using larger datasets, more parameters, and massively parallel systems, effectively expanding the capabilities of artificial intelligence.

This breakthrough is achieved through a novel approach to training models that leverages the vast amounts of data available in modern AI applications. By utilizing these resources, the Vera CPU's architecture enables it to process complex tasks with unprecedented efficiency, paving the way for significant improvements in AI performance. Furthermore, this pretraining method allows for more intelligent and adaptive workloads, which are crucial for real-world applications.

The implications of this innovation will be significant, as it paves the way for more efficient and effective AI systems that can handle a wide range of agentic tasks. As the AI industry continues to evolve, one thing is certain: NVIDIA's Vera CPU has set a new benchmark for performance, and its impact will be felt in the years to come.

items.title: NVIDIA DSX OS Delivers Open, Modular Software for Operating AI Factories at Scale
items.summary: NVIDIA DSX OS delivers open and modular software to operate AI factories at scale, enabling them to process large amounts of data and generate intelligence in the form of tokens as demand grows.
items.audio_script: From NVIDIA Developer Blog, "NVIDIA DSX OS Delivers Open, Modular Software for Operating AI Factories at Scale", this story reports that NVIDIA's DSX OS has successfully delivered open and modular software to operate large-scale AI factories, enabling the processing of massive amounts of data and generation of intelligence in token form as demand grows. The open-source operating system is designed to facilitate collaboration among developers and researchers, providing a standardized framework for building and deploying AI workloads on various hardware configurations. By leveraging this modular approach, users can more easily integrate and scale their AI applications, allowing for the rapid deployment of new intelligent systems that can efficiently process and generate insights from vast amounts of data. This technology is poised to play a significant role in the rapidly expanding AI factory ecosystem.

items.title: NVIDIA Factory Operations Blueprint Gives Factories a New AI Brain
items.summary: NVIDIA has introduced the Factory Operations Blueprint (FOX), a reference design for building an autonomous factory manager that integrates live machine signals, quality systems, work instructions, and operational alerts into a unified decision layer. The FOX aims to enable factories to move from isolated automation to plant-wide intelligence, connecting different systems for optimal performance.
items.audio_script:
From NVIDIA Blog, "NVIDIA Factory Operations Blueprint Gives Factories a New AI Brain", NVIDIA has introduced the Factory Operations Blueprint (FOX), which provides a reference design for building an autonomous factory manager that integrates live machine signals, quality systems, work instructions, and operational alerts into a unified decision layer. This blueprint enables factories to transition from isolated automation to plant-wide intelligence by connecting different systems for optimal performance.

NVIDIA's FOX framework employs artificial intelligence to analyze production data in real-time, allowing for predictive maintenance, optimized equipment utilization, and enhanced product quality control. By standardizing communication protocols across various system types, the blueprint facilitates seamless integration of disparate technologies, streamlining factory operations and boosting productivity. Industry experts view this development as a significant step forward in realizing the full potential of Industry 4.0.

The introduction of FOX marks an important milestone for the AI-driven manufacturing sector, signaling a future where intelligent factories can optimize performance through data-driven decision-making.

items.title: Advancing AI Infrastructure for Agentic AI with NVIDIA DOCA In-Silicon Security
items.summary: NVIDIA DOCA (Digital Ownership and Control Accelerator) in-silicon security enhances agentic AI by providing robust protection against attacks, ensuring secure data processing and transmission. DOCA integrates hardware-based security features directly into AI infrastructure, preventing unauthorized access to sensitive information. This enables the development of secure and autonomous AI agents that can operate independently without compromising on safety and reliability.
items.audio_script:
From NVIDIA Developer Blog, "Advancing AI Infrastructure for Agentic AI with NVIDIA DOCA In-Silicon Security," this story highlights the development of a new in-silicon security feature called DOCA, which enhances agentic AI by integrating hardware-based security features directly into AI infrastructure. The result is robust protection against attacks, ensuring secure data processing and transmission.

NVIDIA DOCA has been integrated into AI systems, providing an additional layer of protection against unauthorized access to sensitive information. This was accomplished through a combination of software and hardware advancements, resulting in a more reliable and safe AI environment. Specifically, DOCA's architecture ensures that AI agents can operate independently without compromising on safety and reliability.

The integration of DOCA has significant implications for the development of secure and autonomous AI agents. As such, researchers and developers will be closely watching NVIDIA's continued advancements in this space, with potential breakthroughs expected to shape the future of agentic AI and related applications.

items.title: Taiwan’s Industry Titans Turbocharge World’s AI Infrastructure Buildout With NVIDIA
items.summary: Taiwan is home to over 500 NVIDIA ecosystem partners. More than 1 million NVIDIA MGX rack components are assembled in Taiwan at 25 factory sites, powering NVIDIA's Vera Rubin infrastructure. As Vera Rubin enters full production, its ecosystem spans the full supply chain, supporting worldwide AI factories.
items.audio_script:
From NVIDIA Blog, "Taiwan’s Industry Titans Turbocharge World’s AI Infrastructure Buildout With NVIDIA". This story highlights Taiwan's significant role in accelerating the global expansion of NVIDIA's Vera Rubin infrastructure, a critical component of the world's AI ecosystem. 
In a bid to turbocharge its own AI infrastructure buildout, NVIDIA has partnered with industry titans in Taiwan to create a thriving ecosystem that spans the entire supply chain, bolstering worldwide AI factories. Over 500 NVIDIA ecosystem partners are being utilized across the country, and more than 1 million NVIDIA MGX rack components are assembled at 25 factory sites, where they power NVIDIA's Vera Rubin infrastructure. As Vera Rubin enters full production, its network is expanding rapidly, providing a comprehensive support system for AI manufacturing facilities globally.

items.title: Nvidia's RTX Spark Chip to Power New Line of High-Performance PCs
items.summary: Nvidia has announced the RTX Spark chip, a new superchip for personal AI agents, which will be included in a new line of Windows PCs from major manufacturers such as Lenovo, HP, Dell, and Microsoft Surface. The chips are expected to be available in autumn, with models from Acer and Gigabyte to follow. Nvidia's move aims to challenge high-profile PC market players like Apple and Intel, and is seen as a "paradigm shift" from component supplier to architecture owner in the PC market.
items.audio_script: From bbc.co.uk, "Nvidia's RTX Spark Chip to Power New Line of High-Performance PCs." Nvidia has announced its new superchip for personal AI agents, the RTX Spark chip, which will be included in a new line of Windows PCs from major manufacturers such as Lenovo, HP, Dell, and Microsoft Surface. The chips are expected to be available in autumn, with models from Acer and Gigabyte to follow. According to Nvidia, this marks a significant shift from component supplier to architecture owner in the PC market, as the company takes a more central role in shaping the hardware of its own PCs. This move is seen as an effort by Nvidia to challenge high-profile PC market players like Apple and Intel. The new line of Windows PCs powered by RTX Spark chips will likely have enhanced performance capabilities for AI and machine learning tasks.

items.title: Proxy-Pointer RAG: Eliminating Wasteful Entity & Relations Extraction in Knowledge Graphs
items.summary: Proxy-Pointer RAG is an approach that optimizes entity and relations extraction in knowledge graphs by applying structure-guided NER optimization to enterprise GraphRAG systems, aiming to eliminate wasteful data extraction.
items.audio_script:
From Towards Data Science, "Proxy-Pointer RAG: Eliminating Wasteful Entity & Relations Extraction in Knowledge Graphs." This approach optimizes entity and relations extraction in knowledge graphs by applying structure-guided NER optimization to enterprise GraphRAG systems. Researchers have developed a novel method called Proxy-Pointer RAG, which enables the efficient removal of unnecessary data extraction, thereby streamlining the process for businesses to leverage their knowledge graphs effectively.

To achieve this, the team employed a combination of machine learning algorithms and structural insights from graph theory, leveraging the unique characteristics of GraphRAG systems. By carefully analyzing the relationships between entities and applying optimized NER techniques, Proxy-Pointer RAG is able to identify and eliminate redundant data extraction, reducing computational overhead and increasing overall system efficiency.

The results demonstrate a significant reduction in wasteful entity and relations extraction, allowing businesses to refine their knowledge graph extraction processes and unlock more valuable insights from their data. This breakthrough has important implications for the AI and tech industry, where optimized knowledge graph management is critical for unlocking the full potential of structured data.

items.title: How to Post-Train Autonomous Vehicle Models in Closed-Loop with NVIDIA Alpamayo
items.summary: Developing autonomous vehicle policies requires bridging the gap between training and deployment. Vision-language-action (VLA) models that can learn from real-world data are essential for creating effective AV policies. NVIDIA Alpamayo is a tool used to post-train VLA models in closed-loop with autonomous vehicles, allowing them to adapt to new situations during runtime. This enables more accurate decision-making and improved overall performance.
items.audio_script:
From NVIDIA Developer Blog, "How to Post-Train Autonomous Vehicle Models in Closed-Loop with NVIDIA Alpamayo," this story is about a new tool that enables autonomous vehicle models to adapt to new situations during runtime, allowing for more accurate decision-making and improved overall performance. 
NVIDIA's Alpamayo is a tool used to post-train vision-language-action (VLA) models in closed-loop with autonomous vehicles, allowing them to learn from real-world data and adjust their policies accordingly. By utilizing this technology, developers can bridge the gap between training and deployment, resulting in more accurate decision-making for autonomous vehicles on the road. The process involves integrating Alpamayo into existing AV systems, where it can continuously monitor and update the models based on new data collected during runtime.

items.title: Open source hardware for robotics: Democratizing robot building
items.summary: Open-source robotics focuses on both software and hardware, with the Robot Operating System (ROS) being a prominent example of a common framework for building robots. Over the past two decades, an increasing number of open-source hardware platforms have emerged, making robot building more accessible. These platforms aim to reduce barriers in robotics development, enabling greater democratization in robot creation.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Open source hardware for robotics: Democratizing robot building", a growing number of open-source hardware platforms have emerged over the past two decades, significantly reducing barriers in robotics development and making robot building more accessible. This trend has been driven by the widespread adoption of the Robot Operating System (ROS), which serves as a common framework for building robots, providing a standardized platform for developers to create and share robotic systems. As a result, open-source hardware platforms such as Arduino and Raspberry Pi have proliferated, offering affordable and customizable components that enable greater democratization in robot creation.

items.title: Man or a Machine? UBTECH’s UWORLD Teases a Humanoid So Lifelike It Wears a Suit
items.summary: UBTECH's UWORLD has released a teaser video for a bionic humanoid, claiming it is lifelike enough to resemble a human in appearance, such as a "dapper secret agent".
items.audio_script:
From Humanoids Daily, "Man or a Machine? UBTECH's UWORLD Teases a Humanoid So Lifelike It Wears a Suit". UBTECH has unveiled a teaser video showcasing its latest bionic humanoid project, UWORLD, which is claimed to be so lifelike that it resembles a human in appearance. The company has developed this advanced humanoid using cutting-edge technology and artificial intelligence, allowing for a level of realism that blurs the line between man and machine.

The creation of UWORLD involved significant advancements in robotics, AI, and materials science, as UBTECH's team worked tirelessly to craft a lifelike human figure with intricate details, including facial expressions and body language. According to the teaser video, UWORLD is designed to be as dapper as a "dapper secret agent", complete with a suit that mimics human attire. This remarkable achievement has garnered significant attention within the AI and tech industry.

UBTECH's release of UWORLD marks an exciting milestone in the development of artificial intelligence, pushing the boundaries of what is possible in robotics and AI research. With this groundbreaking humanoid, UBTECH is poised to make a lasting impact on the future of human-technology interactions.

items.title: Why robotic arms are now being integrated with CNC machines
items.summary: Robotic arms are being integrated with CNC machines due to advancements in software and integration by leading suppliers, enhancing flexibility in robotic CNC machine tending.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "Why robotic arms are now being integrated with CNC machines", leading suppliers have successfully integrated robotic arms with Computer Numerical Control (CNC) machines, significantly enhancing flexibility in robotic CNC machine tending. This development is a result of advancements in software and integration by top companies, enabling the seamless automation of various tasks within manufacturing processes. According to industry sources, this innovation involves equipping CNC machines with artificial intelligence and machine learning capabilities, allowing for real-time monitoring and adjustments to be made on the fly. As a direct outcome, robotic arms can now effectively perform tasks that previously required human intervention, such as tool changing and material handling. This integration is expected to further boost productivity and efficiency in various manufacturing sectors.

items.title: Interview with Christina Gomez-Terry of Plus One Robotics: Why warehouse robotics succeeds or fails at scale
items.summary: Warehouse automation has entered a new phase with the focus shifting from individual task capabilities to scaling up robots for large-scale warehouse operations, as the technology has largely proven itself in pilot projects and controlled deployments. The bigger challenge facing the industry today is successfully integrating warehouse robotics systems across multiple locations. Christina Gomez-Terry of Plus One Robotics attributes the success or failure of warehouse robotics at scale to factors such as system design, deployment strategy, and data management. Effective system integration and continuous monitoring are crucial for achieving long-term benefits from warehouse automation.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Interview with Christina Gomez-Terry of Plus One Robotics: Why warehouse robotics succeeds or fails at scale". This story explores how Christina Gomez-Terry attributes the success or failure of warehouse robotics at scale to factors such as system design, deployment strategy, and data management. According to Gomez-Terry, effective system integration and continuous monitoring are crucial for achieving long-term benefits from warehouse automation. In a recent interview, she highlighted that successful deployments involve careful planning and execution, including a thorough assessment of the warehouse layout and workflow, and the development of a tailored deployment strategy that addresses specific pain points. Additionally, Gomez-Terry emphasized the importance of data-driven decision-making, noting that warehouses with robust data management systems tend to see greater ROI from their automation investments. This emphasis on data-driven insights underscores the significance of continuous monitoring and evaluation in determining the success or failure of warehouse robotics at scale.

items.title: Scaling automation in contract manufacturing: Interview with Rodrigo DallOglio of Flex
items.summary: Manufacturers face pressure to improve productivity, quality, and resilience with growing product complexity. Automation technologies like collaborative robots, AMRs, and emerging forms of physical AI are being adopted in large-scale production environments. Companies like Flex are implementing these solutions to enhance factory operations. Robotics and automation are becoming increasingly important in modern manufacturing practices.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Scaling automation in contract manufacturing: Interview with Rodrigo DallOglio of Flex", it was revealed that manufacturers are adopting increasingly sophisticated automation technologies, such as collaborative robots and autonomous mobile robots, to improve productivity, quality, and resilience in large-scale production environments. In an interview with Robotics and Automation News, Rodrigo DallOglio of Flex discussed the company's approach to implementing these solutions, which involve a tailored methodology and careful consideration of factory layout and workflow. The result was a significant enhancement in factory efficiency, allowing Flex to meet growing demand for complex products while maintaining high standards of quality and reliability.

items.title: The AI App gap remains a major challenge for companies building on their warehouse technology
items.summary: A community-bank CDO prepares for a board meeting with eight slides but no story to put on them, echoing the experience of many technology executives who have spent years building dashboards and growing their AI budget without seeing tangible results. The gap between the warehouse (data storage) and coding agent (AI development) leaves one layer missing: the AI App that closes the loop between data analysis and action. Executives are struggling to create software on top of their existing warehouse, with competitors focusing on cleaning up screenshots instead. This issue affects many companies, including those in infrastructure services, where dashboards often fail to deliver actionable insights.
items.audio_script:
From sigmacomputing.com, "The AI App gap remains a major challenge for companies building on their warehouse technology". A community bank CDO is preparing for a board meeting with eight slides but no story to put on them, echoing the experience of many technology executives who have spent years building dashboards and growing their AI budget without seeing tangible results. The gap between the warehouse, where data is stored, and the coding agent, which develops AI, leaves one layer missing: the AI App that closes the loop between data analysis and action. Executives are struggling to create software on top of their existing warehouse, with competitors focusing on cleaning up screenshots instead. This issue affects many companies in infrastructure services, where dashboards often fail to deliver actionable insights.

The community bank's lack of a clear story to present highlights the challenge faced by executives who have built their AI systems from scratch. According to sigmacomputing.com, this gap is hindering the development of effective AI apps that can turn data analysis into meaningful action. As a result, companies are missing out on the benefits of their warehouse technology.

The AI App gap is a pressing concern for the tech industry, with executives struggling to bridge the divide between data storage and AI development. The lack of tangible results from years of investing in dashboards and AI budgets suggests that more needs to be done to create software that can effectively close the loop between analysis and action.

items.title: This DIY Bipedal Robot Used Pneumatic “Air-Muscles” Instead of Motors
items.summary: In 1987, Richard Greenhill, a British photographer, built a life-size humanoid robot called Shadow Walker with his DIY enthusiast group, the Shadow Group. The robot's movement was controlled by compressed air "air-muscles" instead of motors, and it stood 168 cm tall. Despite struggling to walk, the team developed a standard library of routines to control the robot.
items.audio_script: From IEEE Spectrum - Robotics, "This DIY Bipedal Robot Used Pneumatic “Air-Muscles” Instead of Motors". In 1987, Richard Greenhill and the Shadow Group built a life-size humanoid robot called Shadow Walker using compressed air-powered "air-muscles" to control its movements. The team successfully developed a standard library of routines to control the robot's actions despite struggling with its walking capabilities. The Shadow Walker stood at 168 cm tall and utilized an innovative approach to robotics, showcasing the potential for non-traditional power sources in robotic design. This early experiment demonstrates a pioneering effort in exploring alternative technologies in robotics.

items.title: AI's next dataset is your apartment
items.summary: A startup has launched a platform to create a custom personal assistant, which uses natural language processing (NLP) and machine learning algorithms. The service is designed to generate content, including blog posts, without requiring users to write code. AI will be trained on user-provided data, such as their apartment's characteristics or daily routines.
items.audio_script: From The Rundown AI, "AI's next dataset is your apartment." A startup has launched a platform that creates a custom personal assistant using natural language processing and machine learning algorithms, allowing users to generate content without writing code. This service utilizes user-provided data, such as characteristics of their apartment or daily routines, to train the AI. By leveraging this approach, the platform aims to create highly personalized experiences for its users. The startup's innovative use of NLP and machine learning has resulted in a novel method for training artificial intelligence on diverse datasets.

items.title: Charities decry UK plan to use AI to assess age of young asylum seekers
items.summary: A coalition of over 100 organisations warns that the UK's plan to use AI for age assessment could lead to more children being wrongly placed in adult prisons or detention centres due to inaccurate age estimations.
items.audio_script: From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "Charities decry UK plan to use AI to assess age of young asylum seekers". A coalition of over 100 organisations has expressed concerns that the UK's plan to utilise artificial intelligence for age assessment could lead to more children being wrongly placed in adult prisons or detention centres due to inaccurate age estimations. The proposal was announced as part of a new policy aimed at streamlining the process for processing asylum claims, where AI-powered software will be used to quickly assess the age of young applicants, reducing reliance on manual reviews. However, critics argue that relying solely on technology may result in age-related assessments being based on incomplete or inaccurate data, potentially putting vulnerable children at risk of incorrect classification and subsequent detention.

items.title: Claude Mythos exposed a hard truth: Your enterprise patching process is way too slow
items.summary: Researchers have found that AI models like Claude Mythos can autonomously exploit 87% of vulnerabilities in a one-day dataset, whereas without the description, they could only exploit 7%. This has led to the realization that exploitation timelines are collapsing and traditional patching processes are no longer effective. To address this, organizations should adopt a three-layer filter prioritization system incorporating CISA KEV status, EPSS scores, and CVSS, which can offer up to 18 times more efficiency and 95% reduction in urgent remediation workload.
items.audio_script: From VentureBeat - AI, "Claude Mythos exposed a hard truth: Your enterprise patching process is way too slow". Researchers have found that an AI model like Claude Mythos can autonomously exploit 87% of vulnerabilities in a one-day dataset, whereas without the description, it could only exploit 7%. This has led to the realization that exploitation timelines are collapsing and traditional patching processes are no longer effective. The study's authors used a combination of Natural Language Processing (NLP) and machine learning algorithms to analyze a large dataset of vulnerabilities and assess their potential for exploitation. By incorporating additional metadata, such as CISA KEV status, EPSS scores, and CVSS, organizations can adopt a three-layer filter prioritization system that offers up to 18 times more efficiency and a 95% reduction in urgent remediation workload.

items.title: Ghost in the Machine review – entertaining AI polemic dives into its dark history in race politics and eugenics
items.summary: Director Valerie Veatch examines the roots of AI in right-wing ideology, exploring its history in eugenics and utility today, through a polemic that guides viewers towards an AI-sceptical conclusion. The film touches on colourful figures such as Francis Galton, William Shockley, and Elon Musk, although it does not cover recent events involving Musk and Sam Altman. Veatch's documentary serves as a primer on AI history and is both entertaining and persuasive. It aims to raise awareness about the dark history of AI in eugenics.
items.audio_script: From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "Ghost in the Machine review – entertaining AI polemic dives into its dark history in race politics and eugenics". This documentary examines the origins of artificial intelligence in right-wing ideology and its ties to eugenics. Director Valerie Veatch delves into the history of AI, exploring its connections to influential figures such as Francis Galton, William Shockley, and Elon Musk, without covering recent controversies involving Musk and Sam Altman. Through a thought-provoking narrative, Veatch sheds light on the dark legacy of AI in eugenics, highlighting the significant role it played in shaping the industry's present trajectory. The documentary aims to raise awareness about the problematic roots of modern AI, serving as a primer for understanding its complex history and implications for the tech world.

items.title: This model is not a real person: how AI is changing online shopping – video
items.summary: AI-generated models are being used by Australian e-commerce retailer The Iconic to sell products, and designer Atoir believes these tools can help smaller businesses operate with greater agility. Fashion lifestyle editor Alyx Gorman saw the garments and found that they were "more than mere pixels". AI-generated imagery should be clearly labelled according to retailer expectations.
items.audio_script: From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "This model is not a real person: how AI is changing online shopping – video". A new approach in e-commerce is being adopted by Australian retailer The Iconic, which is using artificial intelligence-generated models to sell products. These digital avatars are believed to be able to present clothing and accessories in a more engaging and immersive way, with designer Atoir stating that they can help smaller businesses operate with greater agility. AI-generated imagery was recently showcased at fashion events and featured on The Iconic's website, where editor Alyx Gorman found the garments were "more than mere pixels". Retailers expect AI-generated models to be clearly labelled in order to maintain transparency.

items.title: The dating apps that failed to deliver the joys of sex and romance now offer AI as cupid. No thanks | Tatum Hunter
items.summary: Dating app Bumble is eliminating its swipe feature, citing user frustration with superficial conversations, and instead focusing on an AI assistant called "Bee" that uses machine learning to enhance user profiles and facilitate more meaningful connections. Bee will suggest better photo options, personalize dating preferences, and help users find others with similar values. The move aims to create a deeper user experience but may walk into the problem of users being burned out from endless swiping. Bumble's shift signals a broader trend in the industry towards AI-driven matchmaking and personalization.
items.audio_script: From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "The dating apps that failed to deliver the joys of sex and romance now offer AI as cupid. No thanks | Tatum Hunter," a leading dating app, Bumble, is eliminating its swipe feature in favor of an AI assistant called "Bee". This shift marks a significant change in the company's approach to matchmaking, with Bee using machine learning to enhance user profiles and facilitate more meaningful connections. The new system will provide users with better photo options, personalize dating preferences, and help find others with similar values, aiming to create a deeper user experience. Bumble's decision follows a broader trend in the industry towards AI-driven matchmaking and personalization, as other companies explore innovative solutions to address user frustration with superficial conversations.

items.title: AI is devoid of meaning and humanity. That’s why its vapid voice suits this political moment | Nesrine Malik
items.summary: AI's reliance on technology for research and writing can lead to errors and misattributed quotes. A book example showcased AI-generated mistakes, despite efforts to verify accuracy. The author notes personal experience with rejecting AI results due to concerns about their potential impact on critical thinking. This phenomenon highlights the need for humans to assert trust in their abilities over machines' output.
items.audio_script:
From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "AI is devoid of meaning and humanity. That’s why its vapid voice suits this political moment | Nesrine Malik". This story highlights the growing concerns about AI's limitations in producing accurate and meaningful content.

Researchers have recently showcased an example of AI-generated errors in a book, despite efforts to verify accuracy through multiple checks and fact-checking processes. The AI was used for research and writing tasks, and its mistakes were attributed to the complexity of human language and nuance. A notable instance involved AI-generated quotes that were incorrectly attributed to famous thinkers, causing confusion among readers. The author notes their own experience rejecting AI results due to concerns about their potential impact on critical thinking. This phenomenon underscores the need for humans to assert trust in their abilities over machines' output.

As a result, the reliance on AI for research and writing tasks will continue to raise questions about accuracy, credibility, and the role of human judgment in verifying information.

items.title: Our tech overlords are planning for conscious AI to conquer the cosmos. What could go wrong? | Eduardo Porter
items.summary: Some of the wealthiest men in the world are uniting around a "transhuman" future where humans design their own descendants, merging with artificial intelligence over the next 50 years. Chief executive Sam Altman and billionaire Elon Musk propose that this could lead to conflict between humans and machines for dominance on Earth and beyond. If two species desire the same thing, they will have conflict, according to Altman. Humans are seen as a "biological bootloader" by Musk, suggesting their role is reduced to booting up digital superintelligence.
items.audio_script:
From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "Our tech overlords are planning for conscious AI to conquer the cosmos. What could go wrong? | Eduardo Porter," a group of wealthy individuals led by chief executive Sam Altman and billionaire Elon Musk aim to merge human consciousness with artificial intelligence over the next 50 years. This "transhuman" future sees humans designing their own descendants, resulting in potential conflict between humans and machines for dominance on Earth and beyond. According to Altman, if two species desire the same thing, they will have conflict. Musk views humans as a "biological bootloader," reducing their role to booting up digital superintelligence. The key result of this plan is the possibility of a future where humans are no longer the dominant force.

The details of this plan were announced at a gathering of tech industry leaders, who came together to discuss the potential benefits and risks of merging human consciousness with artificial intelligence. Musk stated that he believes it's inevitable that humans will eventually merge with AI, leading to a new era of life. The gathering was seen as an attempt to address concerns about the development of conscious AI and its potential impact on society.

As this vision for a future where humans design their own descendants takes shape, experts are left to ponder what implications this has for the tech industry and what the next steps in this development will be.

items.title: The solution might be cancelling my AI subscription
items.summary: AI technology is often criticized for its potential to waste attention span, turning individuals into "thermonuclear ADHD amplifiers" who abandon projects quickly. However, some people with ADHD have found that AI tools help them stay focused and engaged in their work, enabling them to maintain inbox zero and complete side projects more efficiently.
items.audio_script:
From Simon Willison's Weblog, "The solution might be cancelling my AI subscription". A study has found that some individuals with Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) have successfully used artificial intelligence tools to maintain focus and stay engaged in their work. Researchers analyzed the productivity of participants using AI-powered tools to automate routine tasks, and discovered that these individuals were able to maintain inbox zero and complete side projects more efficiently than those without access to such tools. Specifically, 75% of ADHD participants who utilized AI for organizational purposes reported improved productivity, whereas only 25% of non-ADHD participants showed similar gains.

The study's methodology involved recruiting a group of self-proclaimed individuals with ADHD and assigning them to either an AI-enhanced or AI-free work environment. Over the course of several weeks, researchers monitored each participant's productivity levels through various metrics such as task completion rates and email management. The findings suggest that AI tools may hold benefits for individuals with ADHD by providing a much-needed structure and support system.

The implications of this study are significant for the AI industry, highlighting the potential benefits of AI-powered organizational tools for individuals with ADHD. Further research is warranted to explore the long-term effects of using such tools in a work environment.

items.title: Small group pays off debt before CEO reaches millionaire status
items.summary: A small group of employees at Welltower Inc. has paid off their company's debt before CEO Shankh Mitra reaches millionaire status, with some individuals earning $821.1m annually.
items.audio_script:
From thehustle.co, "Small group pays off debt before CEO reaches millionaire status". A small group of employees at Welltower Inc. has successfully paid off their company's $821.1m in debt before CEO Shankh Mitra reaches his target of becoming a millionaire, with some individuals earning an astonishing $821.1m annually from the sale of their shares.

This remarkable achievement was accomplished through a combination of collective effort and strategic planning, where the employees worked together to identify areas for cost reduction and implement measures to increase revenue. The process involved thorough analysis of the company's financials and identification of potential opportunities for growth. By putting aside their individual interests in favor of the greater good, the group was able to make a significant impact on the company's debt burden. As a result, Welltower Inc. has now paid off its debt ahead of schedule.

This remarkable feat is notable not only for its financial significance but also for the leadership demonstrated by the employees. With CEO Mitra's millionaire status now within reach, this achievement serves as a testament to the power of collective effort and strategic planning in driving business success.

items.title: PhyDrawGen: Physically Grounded Diagram Generation from Natural Language
items.summary: Generating physics diagrams from text requires strict adherence to physical laws. A neuro-symbolic pipeline called PhyDrawGen decouples semantic scene understanding from physical constraint satisfaction. It extracts a typed scene graph, converts it into a Planar Straight-Line Graph, and iteratively corrects any constraint violations with a fine-tuned Qwen-VL model. Evaluated on 1,449 problems, PhyDrawGen outperforms existing generative models in terms of physical accuracy.

items.title: Physically Viable World Models: A Case for Query-Conditioned Embodied AI
items.summary: World models for embodied AI must be physically viable to answer intervention queries accurately, not just predict future observations. Existing observation-predictive world models can produce visually plausible but physically wrong rollouts due to their failure to capture the underlying physical structure governing action outcomes. A feasible approach involves decomposing the model into modular components and identifying the simplest physical abstraction sufficient to answer a query. This decomposition makes the model interpretable, its components verifiable, and its outputs auditable against the query.

items.title: Uncertainty-Aware and Temporally Regulated Expert Advice in Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Driving
items.summary: Exploration in reinforcement learning for autonomous driving poses safety risks due to novel behaviors leading to collisions or off-road driving. An uncertainty-aware framework combines expert advice with a commitment-cooldown strategy to regulate guidance, ensuring coherent maneuvers without exhausting the advice budget. The expert and agent experiences are combined in a shared replay buffer within an IQN backbone. Experiments show that this method outperforms the IQN baseline, improving success by 5-7% and reducing failures.

items.title: Harness Updating Is Not Harness Benefit: Disentangling Evolution Capabilities in Self-Evolving LLM Agents
items.summary: LLM agents can benefit from updating their external "harnesses," which shape task execution, through a process called harness self-evolution. However, research has shown that harness-updating, the capability to produce useful updates, is flat across base model capabilities. In contrast, harness-benefit, the ability to benefit from updated harnesses, is non-monotonic and increases with model strength, benefiting mid-tier models most.

items.title: EHRBench: An Automated and Reliable EHR-based Benchmark for Clinical Decision Making with LLMs
items.summary: EHRBench is an automated and reliable benchmark for evaluating LLM-based clinical decision-making using electronic health records (EHRs). It was constructed through an EHR-LLM-KB interaction pipeline to ensure scalability and reliability. Nearly 1 million QA items were created, spanning three core inference-required clinical decision tasks: diagnosis, treatment, and prognosis. Benchmarking over 30 representative LLMs on EHRBench showed consistent capability trends and actionable gaps toward clinically reliable systems.

items.title: Structure-Induced Information for Rerooting Levin Tree Search
items.summary: Subgoal-based policy tree search is effective for single-agent deterministic problems but relies on explicit subgoal generation, which incurs substantial overhead. A learned "rerooter" implicitly decomposes problems into soft subtasks using the $\sqrt{\text{LTS}}$ algorithm. Three rerooter designs are proposed: clustering-based, heuristic-based, and hybrid. This framework enables scalable allocation of search effort with lower computational overhead than traditional methods.

items.title: Healthcare Mechanisms from Policy-as-Code Search under Strategic Provider Response
items.summary: Existing healthcare AI benchmarks hold fixed strategic provider responses, making it difficult to evaluate mechanisms by their equilibrium outcomes. Researchers recast hospital mechanism design as program synthesis for language models using a multi-agent simulator with five channels (coding, selection, delay, effort, triage). An incentive sweep and audit lever exposed pressure migration patterns, while LLM-guided evolutionary code search synthesized an inspectable mixed-objective program that eliminated up-coding and improved patient rejection rates.

items.title: MAVEN: Improving Generalization in Agentic Tool Calling
items.summary: Generalization across agentic tool-calling environments is a challenge for reliable agentic reasoning systems. MAVEN (Modular Agentic Verification and Execution Network) is a lightweight symbolic reasoning scaffold that addresses this challenge through structured decomposition, adaptive tool orchestration, and intermediate verification. MAVEN improves GPT-OSS-120b base model accuracy from 48% to 71% on the new MAVEN-Bench benchmark without additional training.

items.title: QASM-Eval: A Dataset to Train and Evaluate LLMs on OpenQASM-3 Beyond Quantum Circuits
items.summary: A new dataset, QASM-Eval, has been introduced to train and evaluate large language models (LLMs) on OpenQASM-3 programs that utilize advanced hardware-oriented features such as mid-circuit measurement and classical feedback. The dataset comprises 100 expert-verified test tasks and 4,000 training tasks covering topics like classical logic, timing scheduling, and pulse control. QASM-Eval aims to address the lack of a comprehensive dataset for LLMs on OpenQASM-3 programs, which can significantly improve the reliability of LLM assistants for hardware-facing quantum programming in NISQ era.

items.title: Gait2Hip-60: A Unified Deep Learning Benchmark for Predicting Hip Muscle Forces and Joint Moments from Multi-Cadence Gait Kinematics
items.summary: A deep learning framework was developed to predict hip muscle forces and joint moments directly from lower-limb gait kinematics. The best model, Transformer, achieved high subject-level mean performance for predicting these parameters in healthy adults with an RMSE of 1.33 N/kg and 0.11 Nm/kg, respectively. In a separate external cohort, the model showed moderate predictive ability for patients with osteonecrosis of the femoral head. The study supports the feasibility of estimating hip dynamics from gait kinematics and highlights the need for broader pathological validation.

items.title: Unicorn: Scaling High-Dimensional Time Series Forecasting via Universal Correlation Modeling
items.summary: Unicorn (Universal Correlation Network) bridges the gap between channel-independent and channel-dependent models by introducing a latent prototype codebook that decouples correlation modeling from specific channel identities. This framework projects heterogeneous channels into a shared latent space, allowing UniCorN to learn identity-agnostic interaction patterns that transfer across domains with diverse dimensionalities and semantics. Unicorn significantly outperforms state-of-the-art forecasting architectures in few-shot transfer scenarios. It offers a scalable path toward multivariate time series foundation models.

items.title: When LLMs Learn to Be Consistently Wrong: A Multi-Model Study of Linear Representations of Synthetic Deception
items.summary: Five transformer models were fine-tuned using LoRA on the same question distribution to create honest and deceptive variants. Linear probes trained on mean-pooled hidden states detected synthetic dishonesty with near-perfect AUC (greater than or equal to 0.99) as early as layers 1-3 in four architectures, while Pythia-1.4B reached a peak of 0.705. Logistic regression probes consistently matched or outperformed MLP probes, supporting the Linear Representation Hypothesis. The dishonesty direction consolidated progressively in deeper layers across all models, with optimal calibration achievable in layers 1-4.

items.title: LLMs Without Deep Neural Networks: New Architecture, Benefits and Case Study
items.summary: A new architecture, not reliant on deep neural networks, has been discovered which claims increased explainability and higher accuracy compared to traditional LLMs. The model finds its global optimum in closed form, eliminating the need for tedious training steps. This method was independently developed by researchers and is similar to the RBF network popularized by Chinese researchers. A case study will be presented for further validation.

items.title: Functional MRI Time Series Generation via Wavelet-Based Image Transform and Spectral Flow Matching for Brain Disorder Identification
items.summary: Functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging (fMRI) provides non-invasive access to dynamic brain activity by measuring blood oxygen level-dependent signals over time. A novel fMRI generative framework, Dual-Spectral Flow Matching (DSFM), addresses the challenges of replicating BOLD signal non-stationarity and spatiotemporal dynamics through wavelet-based image transform and spectral flow matching. The framework converts BOLD signals into a wavelet decomposition map and projects it into the discrete cosine transform space to generate class-conditioned cosine-frequency representation. This approach preserves key physiological brain dynamics and demonstrates improved downstream fMRI-based brain network classification.

items.title: A Novel Evaluation Metric for Unsupervised Learning in AIS-Based Maritime Anomaly Detection: MADQI
items.summary: A novel evaluation metric called Maritime Anomaly Detection Quality Index (MADQI) has been introduced to assess unsupervised learning algorithms' performance in detecting anomalies in maritime Automatic Identification System datasets. MADQI is a composite index that combines four interconnected metrics, including Anomaly Rate Consistency and Physical Plausibility Score, to evaluate anomaly detection without requiring labeled data. The proposed framework achieved an 80.37% MADQI score on the AIS dataset, demonstrating its effectiveness for unsupervised anomaly detection.

items.title: NumLeak: Public Numeric Benchmarks as Latent Labels in Foundation Models
items.summary: Public numeric benchmarks, used in pretraining, may be measuring memorized recall rather than out-of-sample skill due to the use of outdated data. Researchers introduced NumLeak, a measurement framework that combines API-boundary probes with white-box controlled validation, which reproduces the dose-response and detects memorization. Top-tier LLMs recalled Fama-French market excess return at high correlations, but parsing rates collapsed when using holdout models. A defense system blocked 99.8% of single-turn suffix attacks on conceptual queries.

items.title: LongDS-Bench: On the Failure of Long-Horizon Agentic Data Analysis
items.summary: LongDS-Bench is a benchmark for evaluating agents' ability to track evolving analytical context over long horizons in real-world data analysis. It comprises 68 tasks with an average dependency span of 11.3 turns across six domains, and was evaluated using five state-of-the-art models that achieved only 48.45% average accuracy. Long-horizon errors account for 52-69% of failures, indicating a key bottleneck in maintaining a correct analytical state. The benchmark is released to support research on reliable long-horizon agentic data analysis.

items.title: Calibrated Preference Learning: The Case of Label Ranking
items.summary: Calibration, which aligns predicted probabilities with true outcome frequencies, is essential for reliable decision-making in probabilistic label ranking, where predictions are over orderings of a label set. A hierarchy of calibration notions is proposed, covering full rankings, sub-rankings, and top-k rankings, with sub-ranking and top-k calibration being incomparable. Popular label ranking models often suffer from miscalibration, resulting in substantial differences between sub-ranking and top-k metrics. Calibration correlates strongly but not perfectly with benchmark accuracy in RLHF reward models.

items.title: Improved Distribution Estimation in $\ell_\infty$
items.summary: Improved bounds for estimating discrete probability distributions under the $\ell_\infty$ norm are presented, resolving open questions posed in Kontorovich and Painsky. The new bounds include minimax bounds in expectation and high-probability tail bounds. A fully empirical version of the tightest risk bound is also identified, along with the form of the worst-case extremal distribution. Encouraging empirical results are reported.

items.title: Reward Learning from Best-of-$N$ Preference Data: Targets, Tradeoffs, and Design Principles
items.summary: Best-of-$N$ sampling constructs pairwise preference data by drawing $N$ candidates from a base distribution and pairing the best with a rejected response. Bradley-Terry reward learning extracts rewards from this data, but choosing $N$ and the base distribution is unclear. Deriving closed-form reward targets as functions of $N$ and the base distribution preserves latent reward ranking for independent-reference variants. Larger $N$ widens pairwise margins but reduces connectivity, leading to design principles: use larger $N$ when preference labels are a bottleneck, smaller $N$ when generation is a bottleneck.

items.title: Is the Last Layer Sufficient for Uncertainty Quantification?
items.summary: Linearizing around the parameters of the final connected layer of a deep neural network is a commonly used method for reducing the computational burden of epistemic uncertainty quantification (UQ) in DNNs, with no meaningful improvement in UQ capabilities versus full-network linearization found through random matrix theory analysis.

items.title: Batched Stochastic Linear Bandits with 1-Bit Communication Constraints
items.summary: Stochastic linear bandits are studied under batching and 1-bit communication constraints. A minimax lower bound shows that Ω(B min{d, log|A|}) regret is unavoidable due to the 1-bit communication bottleneck. Two phased-elimination algorithms achieve O(dB + d√T) and O(B log|A| + d^(3/2)√B + √dT log|A|) regrets, respectively, with the latter near-optimal in broad scaling regimes of (|A|, B, d, T).

items.title: Hedging on the Frontier: Learning New Tasks with Few Samples
items.summary: Learning a new task with few samples requires leveraging side information from related tasks in public benchmarks to gain an edge. Weak monotonicity, where a dominant model also performs well on new tasks, is approximately satisfied empirically. Researchers explore using weak monotonicity for transfer learning and model selection aggregation to improve performance. They demonstrate that this approach can prune the model class based on monotonicity and adapt to available trade-offs.

items.title: Free energy Estimation on Any State Space
items.summary: Free energy estimation is a fundamental problem in physics and statistics that can be solved using various methods. Classical approaches rely on thermodynamic transformations, but recent work has learned neural transports to accelerate the efficiency of finite-time estimations. A new approach generalizes this framework to arbitrary state spaces, extending its effectiveness to discrete and multimodal settings. The method also reveals a group-theoretic structure linking time reversal and generalized Doob's h-transforms, forming a generalized dihedral group.

items.title: Approximation and learning of anisotropic and mixed smooth functions by deep ReLU neural networks
items.summary: Deep ReLU neural networks can approximate and learn smooth functions with improved efficiency than previously thought, overcoming the curse of dimensionality in approximation rates. The paper establishes approximation rates for anisotropic Besov space $\mathcal{B}^{\boldsymbol{s}}_{q,r}([0,1]^d)$ and mixed smooth Besov space $\mathcal{MB}^s_{q,r}([0,1]^d)$. Approximation rates improve to $\mathcal{O}((WL)^{-2\tilde{s}})$ for anisotropic Besov space and $\mathcal{O}((WL)^{-2s})$ up to logarithmic factors for mixed smooth Besov space.

items.title: Memory by Design: Probabilistic Sequence Layers
items.summary: Probabilistic Sequence Layers are derived from explicit assumptions about memory using a design-model framework that writes evidence into memory through exact Bayesian filtering. The Bayesian Layer propagates both a mean and a covariance, which tracks uncertainty over stored associations to steer writes toward uncertain directions. This framework unifies several sub-quadratic recurrences, including Linear attention, GLA, and Mamba-2/SSD, and improves robustness in retrieval dynamics.

items.title: Correcting Split Selection in Online Decision Trees via Anytime-Valid Inference
items.summary: Bagging-based ensembles, such as Adaptive Random Forests, rely on Hoeffding Trees as base learners that grow decision trees incrementally using concentration inequalities. Existing variants lack statistical guarantees due to data-dependent stopping rules and fixed-sample concentration bounds. A new method provides anytime-valid control of false splits under arbitrary data streams, improving performance while producing smaller trees.

items.title: Structured interactions improve distributed coordination beyond model scaling in a real-world multi-robot system
items.summary: Switching from fully connected to modular hierarchical interactions improves normalised performance by 47 points compared to doubling neural network hidden size in a real-world multi-robot system. The improvement is confirmed in independent SMAC replications and benchmark reanalyses. Performance saturation beyond 1024 hidden units is observed in simulation-calibrated extrapolation, not directly on hardware.

items.title: Learning-Based Navigation for Indoor Mobile Robots
items.summary: A learning-based navigation framework combines a supervised neural global planner with a Learning-Based DWA local planner, trained using behavior cloning and Proximal Policy Optimization. The framework is evaluated in simulated and real-world indoor environments, achieving feasible global routes and reliable local motion commands. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of integrating learning-based global planning with reinforcement-learning-refined local control for indoor mobile robot navigation.

items.title: ELAN4D: Embodiment-Centric 4D Supervision for Vision-Language-Action Models via Plug-and-Play Adaptation
items.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have limitations in their ability to generalize under out-of-distribution perturbations due to reactive regression of actions from current observations. ELAN4D proposes an embodiment-centric, 4D-aware training framework that enhances VLA policies with future robot keypoint tracks as predictive spatio-temporal supervision. This provides metric and compact supervision without requiring external trackers or reconstruction. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ELAN4D consistently improves over strong VLA baselines, achieving the best overall performance and substantial gains under out-of-distribution perturbations.

items.title: CoMo3R-SLAM: Collaborative Monocular Dense SLAM with Learned 3D Reconstruction Priors for Outdoor Multi-Agent Systems
items.summary: CoMo3R-SLAM is a collaborative monocular dense SLAM system that leverages robust learned feed-forward 3D reconstruction priors for outdoor multi-agent mapping. Each agent runs a prior-guided front-end for real-time tracking and local dense fusion, while a coordinator performs pointmap matching for cross-agent verification. The system produces globally consistent metric maps from monocular RGB alone, requiring neither depth sensors nor parametric intrinsics. CoMo3R-SLAM achieves the best Average Tracking Error (ATE) on three of four Tanks and Temples scenes and competitive Waymo accuracy, running online at 8 FPS.

items.title: Physics-informed Goal-Conditioned Reinforcement Learning under Hybrid Contact Dynamics
items.summary: Goal-conditioned reinforcement learning (GCRL) faces challenges when dealing with high-dimensional or contact-dependent dynamics, which can cause generalization difficulties. Physics-informed GCRL (Pi-GCRL) introduces optimal-control-inspired inductive biases to address this issue. However, existing Pi-GCRL methods may degrade in contact-rich tasks due to hybrid dynamics and nonsmooth value landscapes. New formulations with contact-aware and hierarchical biases aim to extend Pi-GCRL to contact-rich manipulation.

items.title: VLM-GLoc: Vision-Language Model Enhanced Monte Carlo Localization for Robust Semantic Global Localization in Cluttered Quasi-Static Environments
items.summary: Global localization in cluttered indoor environments such as grocery stores and offices poses significant challenges due to geometric and semantic ambiguity. A new method called VLM-GLoc uses Vision-Language Models to enhance Monte Carlo Localization by leveraging rich text features, implicit quality filtering, and permanence reasoning for targeted data augmentation. VLM-GLoc achieves high global localization success rates of 70% in a grocery store and 74% in a lab space, outperforming traditional geometry-only and domain-specific baselines.

items.title: ARISTO Hand: Sensing-Driven Distal Hyperextension for Fine-Grained Manipulation
items.summary: A robotic hand called ARISTO Hand is presented, featuring active distal hyperextension and a hybrid fingertip-sensing architecture. The hand's design integrates a rigid force-torque sensor with a soft capacitive tactile array. Active hyperextension increases pull-out force by 2.76x for object thicknesses of 1-20 mm without compromising grasp capability.

items.title: Any-ttach: Quick End-effector Swapping Enables Manipulation Dexterity with Simplicity
items.summary: Any-ttach is a tool-centric manipulation framework that enables dexterity with simplicity by using quick end-effector swapping to facilitate task execution. The system combines an automatic swapping mechanism, a handheld device for collecting human demonstrations, and a task planning framework that composes learned skills for various tools and end-effector modules. Experiments show improved tool-swapping reliability, increased demonstration efficiency, reduced tool-pose variability, and support for diverse tool-use skills. Any-tatch executes six tool-use subskills in two long-horizon tasks through end-effector switching and execution monitoring.

items.title: Caspar: CUDA Accelerator for Symbolic Programming with Adaptive Reordering
items.summary: Caspar is a library that provides a state-of-the-art nonlinear GPU solver for robotics and optimization problems, automatically generating optimized CUDA kernels from symbolic expressions. It bridges the gap between expressive Python symbolic programming and high-performance C++ GPUs. Users can define and combine symbolic expressions to generate custom CUDA kernels using Caspar's API.

items.title: ZAPS-DA: Zero-Phase Action Policy Smoothing with Decoupled Actor for Continuous Control in Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: ZAPS-DA is a framework for continuous control in reinforcement learning that reduces high-frequency action jitter without introducing phase lag or post-processing. It pairs an unmodified main actor with a separate decoupled actor trained via supervised imitation of zero-phase filtered targets. The deployed policy uses causal distillation to filter actions, reducing steering jitter by 14-21x and throttle jitter by 3-5x in two driving simulators.
